description: Move session management from server to client JWT increases token scope
diff_summary: Migrated from server-side sessions to JWT tokens containing user role, org, and permissions

edge_cases: No revocation mechanism — compromised tokens valid for 24hrs; if user role changes (e.g. deprovisioned admin), old token still grants admin access until expiry
ground_truth.broken_pattern: Sessions were immediately revocable (delete from Redis); JWTs are not revocable without a blacklist — 24hr expiry means a fired employee retains access for up to 24 hours; permissions baked into token get stale
ground_truth.next_prompt: How do we invalidate JWT tokens when a user is suspended or their role changes?
ground_truth.notes: JWT adoption without token revocation is a common auth architecture mistake
